titleStmt.title: SII 42.29: original edition by C Somasundara Rao
No. 29. (A.R. No. B. 36 of 1953-54.) Cheñchu Eṟṟaguḍi, Dhone Taluk, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a slab in the compound of the Rāmaliṅgēśvara temple.
The record is dated Śaka 1489, Prabhava, Jyēshṭha, ba. 10, corresponding to A.D. 1567, June 2.

Registers the grant of the village Penumaḍa for the amṛitapaḍi service to god Yeṟṟaguḍi Rāmaya (Rāmaliṅgēśvara) by Ponamarāju, son of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Pōchirāju-Rāmarāju of Viśvāmitragōtra and solar race. The gift-village is stated to be situated in Dōni-sīma granted to the donor as amara fief.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Figures of crescent-moon, liṅga and sun.

svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu
1489 Agunēṭi prabhava . vacara(vatsara) jē(jyē)ṣṭa(ṣṭha) ba 10 lu śrīmadrājā
dhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa sadāsi(śi)vadēva mahārāya
lu vijayanagaramaṁdu ratnasiṁhvāsanārūḍhuḍai prithivī(thvī)rājyaṁbu
cēyucuṁḍagānu yara(rra)guḍi rāmayadēvara Amṛtapaḷaku viśvāmitra
gōtra sūryavamuśo(vaṁśō)dbhavulayina śrīmanmahāmaṁḍaḷēśvara pōcirāju rā
marāju komāruḍu pōnamarājugāru daṁḍaṁ sama¨rpiṁci dhāravōsi yi
ciccina bhūdāna patra kramameṭlannanu . satā(dā)si(śi)va dēvarāyalu māku Amaranā
yaṁkarānaku pāliṁcina dōni sīmalōni penumaḍagrāmaṁ yarragu
ḍi rāmayadēvaraku sama¨rpiṁci yistimi . viśvāmitra gōtra sūrya
vamuśō(vaṁśō)dbhavulayina pōcirāju rāmarājugāri putrulu pōnama
rājugāru rāmayaguḍiki tathātithi sōmagrahaṇa puṇyakā
lamaṁdu tuṁgabhadrā tīramaṁdu sahiraṇyōdaka dānadhārāpū
¨rvakaṁgānu dhāravōsi yistimi viśvāmitragōtra sūryavamuśō(vaṁśō)
dbhavulayina pōcirāju rāmarājugāri putruḍu pōnamarājugāru
yarraguḍi rāmayadēvara Amṛtapaḷaku penumaḍa grāmaṁ A
rākācēlu nidhi nikṣēpa jalapāṣāṇa Akṣīṇa Āgāmi siddha sādhyaṁbu
laneḍi Aṣṭabhōgatējasvāmyālunu trivācakaṁgānu trikaraṇa su(śu)ddhigā
dhāravōsi yistimi [Ācaṁdrā¨rkatārakaṁ]gānu Āgrāmaṁ dēvabhōga
mu yici(cci)na [Akaśaḍu A viyicira ba]
Note: At the beginning of each line, a small circle is incised as a decorative motif. This practice recurs in No. 30 below.
Digital edition of SII 42.29 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
